changes: Hermes Agent shipped v2026.4.3 on 2026-04-03, which is the cleanest new adjacent-benchmark signal in this run because Hermes is one of the few projects pushing the learning-loop lane hard.
SwarmClaw shipped v1.4.0 on 2026-04-03 after two same-day releases, reinforcing how quickly the orchestration / control-plane branch is iterating.
PicoClaw published v0.2.5 on 2026-04-03 and also cut a fresh nightly on 2026-04-04, keeping the cheap-hardware / edge lane visibly active.
OpenClaw itself did not ship a newer release after v2026.4.2, but the repo stayed hot in the 2026-04-04 snapshot and still sets the ecosystem tempo.
The monitored set still did not need to change: Hermes remains the canonical tracked repo at https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent, and the current positioning map still holds.
whyItMatters: The strongest fresh signal is continued category velocity across multiple lanes at once: the core anchor, the learning-loop benchmark, the edge/runtime-efficiency lane, and the orchestration layer are all still shipping in public.
Hermes matters because it strengthens the case that self-improving memory plus skill-learning is becoming a real differentiator, not just marketing copy.
SwarmClaw matters because it shows the market is still exploring management layers above single-agent assistants, not just lighter rewrites of the base assistant.
Even with that motion, this is still incremental execution inside the existing taxonomy, so map.md, tracked-targets.md, and executive-digest.md stay stable.
